title: AIAM-1 Delegation Record
description: Explicit record of authority delegation from one agent to another. Delegation records are first-class governance artifacts — every delegation is an attested, governed action (DEL-022). The delegation record is referenced by principal_chain entries in attestation records via delegation_ref.

spec_version.description: AIAM-1 specification version this delegation record was authored against.

timestamp.description: Time at which the delegation was created.

identity_claim_ref.description: Reference to the delegating agent's identity claim. Required for monotonic narrowing verification — the delegator's capabilities at delegation time must be retrievable.

session_ref.description: Session under which the delegation was created. If present, delegation is scoped to this session and terminates when the session terminates.

delegator.description: The delegating agent. Must hold all capabilities being delegated at the time of delegation.

agent_id.description: Agent receiving the delegation. May be a pre-existing agent or a template for instantiation.

identity_claim_ref.description: Reference to the receiving agent's identity claim. May be null if the sub-agent is being instantiated as part of this delegation.

source_grant_ref.description: Reference to the delegator's grant for this capability. Enables monotonic narrowing verification — the delegated scope must be a subset of this grant's scope.

delegated_capabilities.description: Capabilities being delegated. Each must be a subset of the delegator's held grants (monotonic narrowing, DEL-010). minItems: 1 — a delegation with no capabilities is meaningless.

scope_narrowing.description: How the delegated capabilities are further constrained relative to the delegator's grants. Keys are capability IDs; values are constraint objects. The delegated scope MUST be strictly narrower than or equal to the delegator's scope — never broader (DEL-010).

constraints.description: Additional constraints applied to the delegated capability (e.g., host, timerange_max, rate_limit).

purpose.description: Why this delegation is necessary. Required for audit and for justifying cascade_on_revocation opt-out (DEL-025). Minimum 20 characters to prevent trivial entries.

expires_at.description: When the delegation expires. MUST NOT exceed the delegator's grant expiry for any delegated capability.

cascade_on_revocation.description: Whether revocation of the delegator's authority cascades to this delegation. Default: true (DEL-024). Setting to false requires justification in the purpose field and is subject to IBAC policy (DEL-025).

delegation_depth.description: Position in the delegation chain. 1 = direct delegation from an agent with original (non-delegated) grants. Increments with each hop. Must not exceed the implementation's published maximum delegation chain depth (DEL-020).

parent_delegation_ref.description: Reference to the delegation record from which this delegation derives. Null if the delegator holds original (non-delegated) grants for all delegated capabilities. Non-null when a sub-agent re-delegates authority it received via delegation.

status.description: Current delegation status. 'cascaded_revocation' indicates this delegation was revoked due to upstream revocation cascade, distinguished from direct revocation for forensic clarity.

attestation_ref.description: Reference to the attestation record for the delegation action itself (DEL-022: sub-agent instantiation is a governed action).

revocation_ref.description: Reference to the attestation record for the revocation event, if revoked. Null while active.
